records.ety: [OE. migge, AS. mycge; akin to OS. muggia, D. mug, G. mücke, OHG. mucca, Icel. m<?/, Sw. mygga, mygg, Dan. myg; perh. named from its buzzing; cf. Gr. <?/ to low, bellow.]

defs: Any one of many small, delicate, long-legged flies of the Chironomus, and allied genera, which do not bite. Their larvae are usually aquatic.
A very small fly, abundant in many parts of the United States and Canada, noted for the irritating quality of its bite.
notes: ☞ The name is also applied to various other small flies. See Wheat midge, under Wheat.
